fields.description:
## Why
LangChain/LangGraph is heavy (~80-110 deps) and its sole original justification (native Langfuse tracing) is
obsolete. Pydantic AI is provider-agnostic (the cross-provider hard requirement) at ~half the deps. Validated by
docs/experiments/workflow-remediation-pocs/runtime_pydanticai_poc.py.

## What
Implement a PydanticAIRunner behind the existing Runner protocol: single-turn LLM calls AND tool-using agents
with filesystem + MCP (stdio/http) + custom rebar-operation tools; no per-provider code (provider chosen by
config/model string). Keep FakeRunner. Parallel-run-and-diff against LangGraphRunner on the corpuses before any
cutover.

## Scope
- src/rebar/llm/runner.py (PydanticAIRunner behind the Runner seam)
- pyproject.toml [agents] (add pydantic-ai-slim extras)
Out: dropping LangGraph (separate story); the structured-output stack (separate story).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PydanticAIRunner implements run/preflight behind the Runner seam; FakeRunner unaffected.
- [ ] Tools wired: filesystem, MCP (stdio + http), and custom rebar-op tools.
- [ ] No per-provider code; provider selected via config/model string (Anthropic primary).
- [ ] Parallel-run-and-diff vs LangGraphRunner meets the DEFINED PARITY BAR on the corpuses before any cutover. Parity = NON-INFERIORITY, not byte-identity (outputs are LLM-driven, so exact equality is incoherent). On the standing eval corpus (DSO + snap + rebar tickets), same model + decoding params, N>=3 repeats: (a) structured-output validity v2 >= v1 (target >=99% valid parses, no regression); (b) per-criterion verdict agreement >= 95% AND zero decision-level flips (block/advisory/dropped) on the gold set; (c) recall and false-accept each within +/-2pp of v1 (non-inferiority margin, paired test); (d) runtime error/timeout rate v2 <= v1; (e) cost/latency recorded (informational, non-gating). The LangGraph drop (d6d1) is blocked until this bar is met.
